rationale: Y independently surfaces nearly the same concerns the human reviewers actually raised — the audio_vae.py:141 config-mutation risk (near-verbatim match to amy-why-3459's inline comment) and the serving_speech.py dialect/language removal (X misses this entirely, despite it being a substantial GT discussion thread with a dedicated test-coverage ask). Y also gives concrete file:line fixes and test suggestions throughout, making it more actionable. X is better calibrated (frames these as non-blocking notes matching the PR's actual APPROVED outcome, and correctly reasons through the encoder-already-constructed nuance) so its precision is a bit higher, but its coverage is thinner and its 'no blockers, approve' framing undersells the same open questions Y catches. Neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
